question: Does the paper report HIV sequences from patient samples?
explain: The paper focuses on in vitro resistance selections and experiments using HIV-1 strains, specifically HIV-1 IIIB and HXB2D, rather than patient-derived samples. The experiments described involve laboratory strains and site-directed mutants rather than sequences obtained from patient samples.
sentences: "The drug-resistant strains were selected by serial passage of HIV-1 IIIB in the presence of increasing concentrations of RAL."; "HIV-1 (IIIB) was provided by Dr. Guido van der Groen (Institute of Tropical Medicine, Antwerp, Belgium)."; "HIV-1 molecular clone pHXB2D was provided by Centre for AIDS Reagents (ARP206; Brussels, Belgium)."

question: Does the paper report in vitro drug susceptibility data?
explain: The paper includes detailed in vitro drug susceptibility data, including the selection of HIV-1 strains resistant to raltegravir (RAL) and the phenotypic cross-resistance of these strains to a panel of integrase inhibitors (INIs). The results section describes the susceptibility of selected HIV strains to various INIs and the effect of specific mutations on drug susceptibility.
sentences: "A parallel IVRS methodology in 96-well plates ( Goethals et al., 2008 ) was used to select six strains with reduced susceptibility to RAL."; "The antiviral activity of RAL was determined."; "The susceptibility of the selected HIV strains to a panel of INIs, namely EVG, PACA, L-870,810, PICA and two INIs with a second-generation resistance profile, MK-2048 and Cmpd G, was determined to establish the degree of cross-resistance."
